mission.title: external-secrets: [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent
mission.description: [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent. This issue affects 34+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
## Description of the bug

External Secrets v0.9.9 installed in EKS does not work with the new [EKS Pod Identity Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/pod-identities.html) feature (alternative to IRSA).

steps.title: Apply the fix for [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent
steps.description:
> Are you saying that this won't work because the pod has already assumed the role using the identity association, and can't then assume another role?

You can use `serviceAccountRef` only with IRSA, not Pod Identity. That's because the Pod Identity agent verifies the identity of the caller (ESO)
```yaml
2023/12/13 09:44:17 Ignoring, HTTP credential provider invalid endpoint host, "169.254.170.23", only loopback hosts are allowed. <nil>
```

steps.title: Confirm [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> Are you saying that this won't work because the pod has already assumed the role using the identity association, and can't then assume another role?

You can use `serviceAccountRef` only with IRSA, not Pod Identity. That's because the Pod Identity agent verifies the identity of the caller (ESO) and hence it can not impersonate a different service account.
codeSnippets: 2023/12/13 09:44:17 Ignoring, HTTP credential provider invalid endpoint host, "169.254.170.23", only loopback hosts are allowed. <nil>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
